svg.title: AI Voice Agent — Adapter Pattern Architecture
svg.desc: All three layers (LLM, Voice, Telephony) built with swappable adapters via a single config file

g.text: One file controls all provider switches

svg.text: Core Agent Logic (never changes)
Conversation flow · prompt builder · call state machine
LLM Adapter
Voice Adapter
Telephony Adapter
↓ plug in any provider below — same interface, just swap config ↓
LLM providers (switch via config — same prompt sent to all)
OpenRouter = single API key, routes to 100+ models — best fallback hub for MVP
Voice providers — STT + TTS (swap independently)
STT
TTS
If ElevenLabs quota hits → auto-switch to PlayHT. If Deepgram hits → switch to Whisper (free, local)
Telephony providers (outbound calls + warm transfer)
All support outbound calls + warm transfer. Start Twilio → fallback Telnyx (cheaper at ₹0.004/min)
How the adapter works in code — same interface, any provider
# config.yaml
llm_provider: openrouter # switch → claude / gpt4o / kimi / github
llm_model: meta-llama/... # any model on OpenRouter
stt_provider: deepgram # switch → whisper / assemblyai
tts_provider: elevenlabs # switch → playht / azure
telephony: twilio # switch → telnyx / vonage / sip
fallback_llm: gemini-flash # auto fallback if quota hit
fallback_tts: playht # auto fallback if elevenlabs quota hit
fallback_stt: whisper-local # free, no quota
fallback_phone: telnyx # if twilio credit runs out
Auto quota guard — no manual switching needed
yes
Core agent never knows which provider ran — adapters handle it transparently
